reasoning: Monthly point-bas retention alarm tests conducted and verified OK. Technical compliance confirmed.
Bath temperature sensors and thermal safety cutoffs verified OK by TRESCAL. Periodic controls compliant.
PAC qualification for Line G extension verified. Modification deemed notable but non-substantial. Future inspection will verify conformity.

DAI (automatic fire detection) still absent despite prior commitment for end 2022. MED threat mentioned in prescription. Temperature sensor alarms raccordés since Nov 2022 but generalized DAI not yet deployed.
DAI generalized not yet installed despite 50k€ budget. Six-month deadline set. MED threat explicit.
Smoke extraction for painting cabins 4-5-6 corrected (attestation MISO 22/11/2022). NC from prior inspection lifted.
PM2I quinquennial checks conducted by APAVE for A31 and cuvette. Minor fissure noted with 1-year repair deadline. Compliant overall.
Fire water supply deficit: 3 public hydrants only provide 130+100+150 m3/h individually but no simultaneous test done. Need 360 m3/h. MED threat raised.
Topographic study confirms 1000m3 containment capacity available on site for fire water. Requirement is 936m3 with DAI. Compliant.
Chemical storage checks: no H2O2 >35% or HNO3 >53%, solvent container CF-2h, TGBT extinction system verified OK, GRV stacking compliant.
Point-bas retention alarms, bath level sensors, thermal cutoffs all verified quarterly. All controls current. Functional test confirmed OK.
TVDI retention area: incompatible chemicals (acids/bases/cyanides) properly separated on individual mobile retentions. No compatibility risk.
Cadmium discharges 90-170 µg/l vs target 25 µg/l. No technically viable solution available by Jan 2023 deadline. 10-month extension requested and accepted. MED threat if not resolved.

COHV contamination (perchloroethylene from former degreaser 15 years ago) confirmed in soil gas, groundwater. Puisard 1 in direct contact with stormwater network. Active pollution source in Zone 1. Treatment by activated carbon filtration planned. Off-site pathway via stormwater drainage is a direct threat.
H+ VLE exceedance on stack 20 (OAD 4M). Missing parameters (NOx, NH3, CN, Ni, HF) on multiple stacks. Measurements not done under normal conditions per APAVE. MED threat.
Capture systems for all heated active baths observed. All connected to atmospheric stacks. No diffuse emissions observed.
Number of atmospheric discharge points minimized and consistent with number of treatment lines. Compliant.
Chimney outlets observed: vertical discharge, no obstacles, proper dispersion configuration. Compliant.
No dilution of effluents observed. All pipes connected upstream to capture zones. Compliant.
Measurement points non-conforming vs NF EN 15259 for several stacks (insufficient straight lengths). APAVE does not assess representativity impact. Justification required.
Gas scrubber and dévésiculeur maintenance deficiencies: controls not done at required frequency, no traceability of dévésiculeur visual checks. Maintenance gaps documented.
Measurement methods comply with normalized reference methods. Compliant.
BSD Trackdéchets used since Jan 2022 for both produced and received dangerous waste. Procedures updated. Multiple BSDs checked: no anomalies.
Waste refusals properly traced in Trackdéchets with motifs. Partial refusal tonnage limitation noted as system constraint.
RNDTS correctly populated via Trackdéchets automatic data transfer. Compliant.
Chronological waste register via Trackdéchets feeding RNDTS automatically. No anomaly.
No terres excavées/sédiments currently concerned. Prescription noted for future COHV remediation.
RNDTS terres excavées not applicable currently. Future COHV excavation will trigger requirement.
No catalyseurs usés as products admitted yet, only as déchet code 16 08 03. Within authorized capacity.
TVDI admission records checked by sondage. BSD, visual/olfactory checks, analyses, CAP references all compliant for 2 sampled admissions.
TVDI exploitation register (quantities received, reagents, boues, GIDAF) maintained monthly and compliant.
TVDI waste codes and quantities per GEREP compliant with APC September 2022 authorized list. Capacity of 240 t/j not exceeded.
Fire water still deficient: 3 hydrants provide confirmed individual 60m3/h but no simultaneous test yet. Forage study needed. MED threat reiterated. Recurring issue from multiple inspections.
COHV report AMDE received. Activated carbon treatment for puisards planned July 2023. Zone 1 source decontamination measures pending further EQRS investigations. Progressing but not resolved.
H+ VLE exceedance on stack 20 still unexplained (no dysfonctionnement identified). Missing parameter measurements justified for absence in gas. New measurement planned Q3 2023.
NF EN 15259 non-conformances in measurement points justified by APAVE (aeraulic conformant, no impact on representativity). NC from prior inspection lifted.
Gas scrubber and dévésiculeur maintenance controls now documented and properly traced. Prior inspection NC lifted.
PM2I quinquennial checks verified via GMAO. Oxydation on C16 noted with remediation action scheduled. Annual verifications also conducted. Compliant.
DAI (automatic fire detection) finally installed and operational since September 2023 (DAI from 2021 inspection demand). Long-standing NC resolved.
DAI installed but PV from SIEMENS does not detail zones covered or asservissements. No test of ventilation stoppage demonstrated. Deadline 1 month to justify.
Smoke extraction for painting cabins verified in 2024 (trappe constatée). 2.6% opening ratio confirmed. Compliant.

Fire water still deficient after years of requests. SIEA confirms only 140-150 m3/h simultaneous (vs 360 m3/h required). Multiple urban planning constraints for water reserve. MED proposed to Prefect. Recurring failure since 2021 inspection.
Cadmium emissions reduced below 25µg/l since July 2023 (lamellaire decantation + optimized coagulation/floculation). Multi-year conformity effort resolved.
COHV: regular reporting not transmitted since end 2023. 2 new deep piezometers planned by Nov 2024. Continued investigation needed. Reporting gap is primary concern.
2023 atmospheric emissions: OH- (113 vs 10 mg/m3) and H+ (116 vs 0.5 mg/m3) exceedances on 2 stacks. Unexplained. Next control December 2024.
Sampling point analysis for NF EN 15259: aéraulic conformant, non-conformances on straight lengths do not affect representativity. Point addressed.
2023 VLE exceedances (OH- and H+) still unexplained. Missing parameters justification transmitted. Future PAC modification for monitoring adaptation needed.
Plan de défense contre l'incendie incomplete per arrêté 22/12/2023 (applicable 01/07/2024). New content requirements (POI, FDS access, training justification etc.) must be integrated.
Automatic detection system installed and operational. Exercise conducted September 2024 with compte rendu. Compliant.
Stock management software tracks nature and quantity of all waste by GRV reference. Daily update for dangerous waste. 400-550 t/month within 240 t/j limit.
PFAS list not established as required by arrêté 20/06/2023. 1-month deadline to establish list of PFAS used, produced, treated or discharged.
3 PFAS analysis campaigns conducted (Jan-Mar 2024). Results: mostly <LQ, one AOF at 7.7 µg/l. Forage also analyzed (all <LQ). Compliant with campaign requirement.
SGS Environmental Analytics accredited under RvA n°L028 (EN ISO/IEC 17025:2017). Laboratory qualification compliant.
SGS report states sampling descriptions from client only. Unclear if SGS conducted sampling themselves. Justification that samples were taken by accredited laboratory required within 1 month.
PFAS quantification limits: AOF LQ=2 µg/l, PFAS LQ=100 ng/l. Both within regulatory requirements.
PFAS campaign results transmitted to GIDAF within regulatory deadlines. Compliant.
Forage withdrawals: 18742 m3 in 2023 (down 19% from 2018). Volume tracking compliant. Eco-Flux diagnostic ongoing targeting 25% reduction by 2025.
Specific water consumption for TS not calculated (difficulty estimating treated surfaces). Annual calculation required. 1-month deadline.
Cuve on line D mislabeled (wrong color code) causing apparent chemical incompatibility with associated retention basin. Labeling correction required.
Confinement discharge point not located or signaled. Balloon obturateur available but operationality not demonstrated. 1-month deadline for justification.
3 non-conforming GRV stored outside reinforced waterproof area for ~1 month. Immediate corrective action required.
MED from December 2024 lifted. 480m3 flexible bâche installed (SDIS confirmed only 2 simultaneous hydrants needed). Reserve in water day of inspection. Pending: SDIS aspiration test.
2 deep piezometers installed. Feb 2025 campaign: plume limited to site with some TCE traces off-site in PP2 (to confirm). Charcoal active treatment operational. Further monitoring recommended.
2024 atmospheric control: HF exceedances on stacks 6 and 17 (different parameters than 2023). New scrubber tower under consideration. Prior OH-/H+ non-conformances not reproduced.
PDI updated but needs integration of new 480m3 water reserve. Document physically available at site entrance. Remote digital access system pending (onduleurs).
PFAS list established. AOF above LQ in one campaign. Waste intake protocols updated (PTFE rejected from Jan 2025). Multiple investigations ongoing. AOF source not yet identified.
2023 specific water consumption communicated (7.8 l/m2). 2024 calculation not yet done. Annual requirement. 1-month deadline.
Discharge point now signaled. Obturateur system described in PDI but no operational exercise done. Difficult access (fossé in water). Vanne automatique de coupure study underway.
Non-conforming GRV now on étanche zone but fissures observed on reinforced étanche floor. Periodic repairs done but formal restitution required.

Change of operator (Fondaudège Pressing to L&O Dyomin) not declared. Activity actually stopped Nov 2020 (machine destroyed). Cessation declaration submitted day of inspection. ATTES SECUR required to finalize cessation (rubrique 2345 requires it).
Physical verification: no dry cleaning machine, no perchloroethylene stock. Activity is ironing only. Site effectively ceased for the classified activity.
Cessation declaration registered. ATTES SECUR (certification by approved firm for security measures per L.512-12-1) still required to complete the cessation process.
New distillery: chai 2 not yet built, only 2 alambics (3rd planned). Storage well below authorized 499.9m3. Conformant with current authorization for current state.
Fire access road (voie engins) on 2+ facades with required dimensions verified. Turnaround zone present. Compliant.
Retention (caniveau siphons, basin 270m3, geomembrane), threshold sills on doors. Monthly water level checks on siphons. Compliant.
300m3 fire reserve with dual connection for 2 SDIS engines (120m3/h capacity). Requirement is 60m3/h for 2 hours. Largely compliant.
Storage height ≤5m, cuve degrees <70°, pressure vents dimensioned correctly, REI 240 walls with correct toiture overhang, EI 30 doors. Compliant (chai 2 distance not verifiable yet).
Two process water networks with siphon fire stops. 380m3 effluent basin. REI 120 between distillery and tasting room. DOE confirms REI 120 facades. Compliant.
Truck unloading area connected to 30m3 underground retention tank (volume not verified by inspection). Prescribed to prevent flammable alcohol pool formation.
First inspection: no written evacuation procedure for tasting room occupants. Muster point identified but not posted. 3-month deadline. MED threat if not resolved.
Off-hours surveillance incomplete: video OK but anti-intrusion system not yet installed (pending). 3-month deadline. MED threat.
Smoke extraction (DENFC) manual-only at distillery and chai 1. No automatic triggers. 3-month deadline for automatic commands or equivalent measures. MED threat.
New installation (end 2022): Q18/Q19 electrical and thermographic verification contracted with ANCO for 2023. Will be addressed at next inspection.
Grounding absent on alambics and barrel racks. Prise de terre only on trucks at unloading, cuves inox, gas pipeline. IP55 equipment verified OK. 1-month deadline for full grounding. MED threat.
Extinguishers (≥2 per local at 144B) present, visible and accessible. Compliant.
ATEX not mastered: ATEX study outdated (references GPL not installed, wrong storage volumes). No 'Ex' signage, no DRPCE, no equipment certification. 3-month deadline for full compliance. MED threat.

ANCO audit identified multiple NCs: ATEX signage, CF-30 partition justification, DENFC sizing, drainage issues, DOE missing. ANCO audit itself incomplete (ATEX article incorrectly states no explosion risk). New full audit required by mid-May 2023. MED threat.
Fire water reserve recalculation completed (SDIS engagement). PAC to be filed mid-2023 integrating extension. Prior inconsistency (650 vs 480 m3/h) addressed.
SDIS letter establishes conditions for using lixiviat basin as fire reserve (480m3 with DAI and astreinte, or 240m3 with additional means). Operator to justify via PAC.
RIA maintenance tracking established since May 2021. Register to be sent to inspection within 1 month. Compliant in principle.
Lixiviat basin accepted as fire reserve under SDIS conditions. Prior NC about needing additional reserve moot.
Basin overflow corrected since last inspection. No overflow observed day of inspection.
Groundwater monitoring (ammonium, DCO) tracking requested. Operator has done monthly monitoring since Feb 2021. Graphical summary to be sent within 2 months.
Complaints register maintained, no complaints received since last inspection. Odor management procedure to be included in SME.
Leachate treatment strategy being tested (reed filters, activated carbon). Decision to be integrated in PAC mid-2023.
Merlon made of sorting rejects placed directly on unpaved ground (not on étanche dalle as required). Operator to clarify origin of requirement and address in PAC.
Plastic content in compost (pots from public waste) within NFU 44-051 norm (<5% impurities). Bordeaux Métropole to be informed for déchetterie sensitization.
Compost heap extends onto grass area outside étanche dalle. Runoff not collected as prescribed. 6-month deadline to collect all runoff in prescribed collection network.
Installation conforms to declaration (récépissé 2017). First item verified: compliant.
Separate wastewater/stormwater networks with bypass at pressing zone. Compliant.
No groundwater discharge. Effluents collected in 140hl underground tank then collected by CUMA. Compliant.
12 dogs present plus 3 personal. Count verified against classification threshold.
Installation located inside residential lotissement, <100m from neighboring habitations (mandatory distance). Buildings in prohibited zone. Structural NC.
Site well maintained. Compliant.
Animal housing (boxes) well maintained, hard materials, daily cleaning and disinfection. Parc d'ébat maintained. Compliant.
Wastewater connected to collective sewer without authorization (L.1331-10 CSP). Effluent discharge requires formal authorization which is absent.
No barking observed during inspection. Isolation materials in buildings. But simple grillage on property boundary allows visual contact with public road, potentially stimulating barking.
New wood processing machines added without PAC notification. 30-day deadline to declare new equipment with installed power details.
Fire team formation not done in 2021-2022. Non-compliance with prescription. 30-day deadline for next exercise scheduling. MED exposure mentioned.
Two water reserves both at wrong location (both South instead of one North+one South). Access path to South reserve not stabilized. No PI debit attestation. Recurring non-conformance from 2014 MED.
Wood treatment equipment and safety organs (alarms, floats, retentions) inspected without particular remark. Compliant.
No atmospheric measurements done since 2015 (cyclone recently replaced, adjustments underway). Required every 3 years per prescription. 3-month deadline. MED exposure.
No 2662 polymer currently stored. Enregistrement maintained. Other rubriques unchanged from 2021 AP. PAC obligation if polymers resume.
Stock management software lacks ICPE rubric column. Quantities by rubrique not quickly determinable. Update required.
Spot check: no 2662 products found in building B as prescribed. Compliant.
Storage conditions generally OK. Liquid GRVs present in building A not accounted for in D9A calculation, but 400m3 confinement well below 1200m3 available capacity. Update calculation requested.
Storage in building A: aisle widths, island areas, height limits all verified OK.
Building B: only 1 manual smoke extraction command present (2 required at opposite ends). 3-month deadline for second command installation.
Detection system apparently functional but verification report not available during inspection. Report required with any corrective action justification.

Private hydrant insufficient debit. Convention with Foresa neighbor for their reserve, but volume and debit not documented. Required: 480 m3/h for 2 hours. Justification or complementary measures needed.
Extinguishers verified OK. RIA verification report and implantation plan for building B absent. Minimum RIA count conformity cannot be verified.
Production 26000 m3/year with unchanged 2m3 malaxage capacity (below 3m3 threshold for enregistrement). Declared production 25000m3/year in declaration.

MED on effluent treatment lifted: cuve tampon 47m3 installed, decantation circuit completed, pH treatment (CO2) and filtration added. All equipment verified operational. Modification declared June 2022. Some justificatory docs still pending.
Water consumption 276 l/m3 below 350 l/m3 limit. Internal target 240 l/m3. Compliant.

pH control: exceedances in May 2023 (9.4) and Sept 2022 (10.9) vs limit 9.5. Measurement point representativity questionable (before final treatment). Performance justification reiteraterd with 1-month deadline.
Total cessation declared March 2022. Only dragline used, no hazardous products, no water extraction/discharge, no incidents. Groundwater monitoring bilan clean. No residual effects.
Site restoration observed: islands, sinuous banks, low-slope shores, plantations, natural vegetation. Jussie (invasive plant) noted. Coordinated with prior quarry restorations.
Défrichement done avoiding protected Picampeau stream forest. Indemnity of 35622€ paid to fonds stratégique (DRFIP justificative). Compliant.
Access from RD17E1: tourne-à-gauche created, Tripoteau hamlet bypass road built. Speed limitation recommended (curve geometry). Compliant.

Fence partially installed (along RD17E1). Sensitive zone balisage done with stakes during défrichement. East limit not yet completed. Simethis ecology firm coordinating. Justification of avoidance measures requested.
